sections.paragraph:
- Original: Ἰούλιος

- Transliteration: Ioulios

- Phonetic: ee-oo'-lee-os

- Definition: Julius = "soft-haired"

1. the centurion of "Augustus Band", to whose charge Paul was delivered when he was sent prisoner from Caesarea to Rome

- Origin: of Latin origin

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Of Latin origin; Julius a centurion: - Julius.
